manual_entries.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm
manual_entries.summary: If measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0.

structured_payload.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm
structured_payload.summary: If measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Measured airflow below threshold for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Generate alarm at specified level; suppress if Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

5.6.5.4.  In Occupied Mode , the heating coil shall be modulated to maintain a DAT  no lower than 10°C
(50°F).
This prevents excessively cold DATs  if the AHU is providing high outdoor airflows and does not have a heating coil.

chunk.cleaned_text:
If ventilation is according to California Title 24, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling
when the primary air volume is less than Zone -Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off
when primary air volume is above Zone -Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by
the primary air at minimum setpoint  meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
5.7.6.  Alarms
5.7.6.1.  Low Primary Airflow
a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone..

is the heating maximum endpoint  and whose output is the maximum hot duct damper
position ranging from 0% to 100%.

damper position to Vheat -max. When this comes into pla y, the only air passing through the discharge airflow sensor
is heating air.

(5°F), generate a  Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload.title: Leaking Damper Alarm
structured_payload.summary: If damper position 0% and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of Vcool-max, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan proven ON, generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Damper closed, fan ON, airflow above threshold for 10 minutes.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

chunk.cleaned_text:
If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minute s while the fan serving the
zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
The constant  value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
5.5.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  damper full closed/open , and
e. reset  request -hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).
Per Section 5.1.11 , all hardware points can be overridden through the BAS.

transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
5.9.6.5.  Leaking Damper. If the damper position is 0%,  and airflow sensor reading is above the la rger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the
zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
5.9.6.6.  Leaking Valve.  If the valve position is 0% for 15 minutes , and DAT  is above AHU SAT by 3°C
(5°F), generate a  Level 4 alarm.
5.9.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides .

reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.
5.12.6.2.  Airflow Sensor Calibration.  If the fan s erving the zone is OFF and airflow sensor reading is above
the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3
alarm.
5.12.6.3.  Leaking Damper.

structured_payload.title: Leaking Damper Alarm

structured_payload.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 alarm.

chunk.cleaned_text:
If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minute s while the fan serving the
zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
The constant  value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
5.5.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  damper full closed/open , and
e. reset  request -hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).
Per Section 5.1.11 , all hardware points can be overridden through the BAS.

manual_entries.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm
manual_entries.summary: If measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If < 50% for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress if zone has Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R loop.

structured_payload.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm
structured_payload.summary: If measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0, generate Level 4 alarm. If < 50% for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress if zone has Importance-Multiplier = 0 for static pressure reset T&R loop.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Measured airflow < 70% or < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes with setpoint > 0.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Generate Level 4 or Level 3 alarm accordingly; suppress for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

chunk.cleaned_text:
If ventilation is according to California Title 24, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling
when the primary air volume is less than Zone -Abs-OA-min for 1 minute, and shall shut off
when primary air volume is above Zone -Abs-OA-min by 10% for 3 minutes.
The designer must ensure that the sum of the indirect ventilation provided by the fan plus the ventilation provided by
the primary air at minimum setpoint  meet Standard 62.1 requirements.
5.7.6.  Alarms
5.7.6.1.  Low Primary Airflow
a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.
b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.
c. If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone..

zone is proven on, generate a Level 4 alarm.
The constant  value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
5.5.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a system -level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  damper full closed/open , and
e. reset  request -hours accumulator point to zero (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).
Per Section 5.1.11 , all hardware points can be overridden through the BAS.

manual_entries.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
manual_entries.summary: If zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes (after suppression per 5.1.20), send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until Cooling Loop < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

structured_payload.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload.summary: If zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes (after suppression per 5.1.20), send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until Cooling Loop < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature or cooling loop output thresholds.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send specified number of requests based on conditions.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Secti on 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.

85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.

manual_entries.title: Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit System Requests - Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset
manual_entries.summary: Send 3 requests if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint while setpoint > 0 and damper > 95% for 1 minute; else send 2 requests if airflow < 70% of setpoint with same conditions; else send 1 request if damper > 95% until < 85%; else send 0 requests.

manual_entries.title: Dual-Duct VAV Cooling SAT Reset Requests
manual_entries.summary: If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes (after suppression), send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Duct VAV Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload.summary: If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes (after suppression), send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature deviation or loop output high
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send integer request count to system level for SAT reset.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

structured_payload.summary: If zone temperature exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes (after suppression), send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until loop < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

manual_entries.title: Low-Discharge Air Temperature Alarm
manual_entries.summary: If heating hot-water plant proven ON and DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 4 alarm. If DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0.

structured_payload.title: Low-Discharge Air Temperature Alarm
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit with heating coil
structured_payload.summary: If heating hot-water plant proven ON and DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 4 alarm. If DAT is 17°C (30°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm. Suppress alarms for zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Heating plant ON and DAT below threshold for 10 minutes.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Generate alarm at specified level; suppress if Importance-Multiplier = 0.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If heating hot-water plant is proven ON, and the DAT is 8.3°C (15°F) less than setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

Level 3 alarm.

Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit  − Snap -Acting Control
Snap -acting control  logic is the first choice among the various DD control schemes , as it is the most efficient and
does not require DD boxes with mixing sections that have a high pressure drop. It allows use  of dual standard
airflow sensors, one at each inlet, with standard pressure independent logic blocks; alternatively, a single discharge
airflow sensor may be used.
However, snap -acting logic is not ideal for CO 2 control because it can cause the zone to os cillate between cooling
and heating.

manual_entries.title: Zone State Heating - Discharge Temperature Reset
manual_entries.summary: During heating, primary airflow setpoint is minimum endpoint. Heating loop output (0-100%) resets discharge temperature from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint. Heating coil modulates to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Zone State Heating - Discharge Temperature Reset
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit with constant-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: During heating, primary airflow setpoint is minimum endpoint. Heating loop output (0-100%) resets discharge temperature from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint. Heating coil modulates to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating
structured_payload.required_behavior: Active primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint. As heating-loop output increases from 0% to 100%, reset discharge temperature from current AHU SAT setpoint to a maximum of MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint. Modulate heating coil to maintain discharge temperature at setpoint.
configurable_values: MaxΔT (maximum DAT rise above heating setpoint)
structured_payload.evidence_quote: As the heating-loop output increases from 0% to 100%, it shall reset the discharge temperature from the current AHU SAT setpoint to a maximum of MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint.

manual_entries.title: Fan Control Logic for Parallel Fan-Powered Terminal Unit
manual_entries.summary: Fan runs during heating. For ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation, fan runs in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume < Voz for 1 minute, shuts off when primary air volume > Voz by 10% for 3 minutes. For California Title 24, similar logic with Zone-Abs-OA-min.

structured_payload.title: Fan Control Logic for Parallel Fan-Powered Terminal Unit
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered terminal unit with constant-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: Fan runs during heating. For ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation, fan runs in Deadband and Cooling when primary air volume < Voz for 1 minute, shuts off when primary air volume > Voz by 10% for 3 minutes. For California Title 24, similar logic with Zone-Abs-OA-min.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone State is heating, or (in Deadband/Cooling) primary air volume < Voz (or Zone-Abs-OA-min) for 1 minute
structured_payload.required_behavior: Fan runs when Zone State is heating. In Deadband and Cooling: if ventilation per ASHRAE 62.1, fan runs when primary air volume < Voz for 1 minute, shuts off when primary air volume > Voz by 10% for 3 minutes. If per Title 24, use Zone-Abs-OA-min instead of Voz.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Fan shall run whenever Zone State is heating. If ventilation is according to ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016, the fan shall run in Deadband and Cooling when the primary air volume is less than Voz for 1 minute and shall shut off when primary air volume is above Voz by 10% for 3 minutes.

structured_payload.title: Fan Status Alarm

manual_entries.title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit Control Logic
manual_entries.summary: In cooling mode, cooling loop output maps to primary airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum, and to series fan airflow setpoint from max(OA-min, minimum endpoint) to cooling maximum; heating coil OFF. In deadband, primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint, series fan airflow setpoint = OA-min, heating coil OFF. In heating, from 0-50% heating loop output resets discharge temperature setpoint from AHU SAT to max MaxΔT above space temp, primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint, series fan airflow setpoint = OA-min; from 50-100% heating loop output resets series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax, primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint; heating coil modulates to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit Control Logic
structured_payload.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Variable-Volume Fan
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered VAV zone with variable-volume fan
structured_payload.summary: In cooling mode, cooling loop output maps to primary airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum, and to series fan airflow setpoint from max(OA-min, minimum endpoint) to cooling maximum; heating coil OFF. In deadband, primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint, series fan airflow setpoint = OA-min, heating coil OFF. In heating, from 0-50% heating loop output resets discharge temperature setpoint from AHU SAT to max MaxΔT above space temp, primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint, series fan airflow setpoint = OA-min; from 50-100% heating loop output resets series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax, primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint; heating coil modulates to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone state changes (cooling, deadband, heating) based on zone temperature relative to setpoints.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Follow mapping of loop outputs to setpoints as described; modulate VAV damper to maintain measured airflow at setpoint; fan runs when zone in heating/cooling or Zone Group occupied; prior to fan start, drive damper fully closed, then release after 15s fan proven ON.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: In the heating Zone State, the logic keeps the fan airflow rate low while supply air temperature is increased as the first heating stage.

82 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  Table 5.8.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
5.8.5.  Control logic is depicted schematically in Figure 5.8.5  and described in the following subsections.  In
Figure 5.8.5 , OA -min is Voz (if using ASHRAE Standard 62.1 ventilation logic) or Zone -Abs-OA-min (if
using California Title 24 ventilation logic).
Figure 5.8.5 Control logic for variable -volume parallel fan -powered VAV zone.
In the heating Zone State , the logic keeps the fan airflow rate low while supply air temperature is increased as the
first heating stage. This presumes that the temperature of the air the fan is supplying is neutral or below the space
temperature, as it would be if the fan draws air directly from the space , and as it might be if the fan draws air from
a return air plenum that is cooled by roof and wall heat losses .

evidence.evidence_text: In the heating Zone State, the logic keeps the fan airflow rate low while supply air temperature is increased as the first heating stage.

chunk.cleaned_text:
90 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021
Figure 5.10.5 Control logic for variable -volume series fan -powered VAV zone.
In the heating Zone State , the logic keeps the fan airflow rate low while supply air temperature is increased as the
first heating stage. This presumes that the temperature of the air the fan is supplying is neutral or below the space
temperature, as it wo uld be if the fan draws air directly from the space , and as it might be if the fan draws air from
a return air plenum that is cooled by roof and wall heat losses. In the past, return air plenums were warmed by
recessed light fixtures, but pendent  lights ar e increasingly  common , so the potential for free heating from the plenum
is smaller than it was. Because  there is the potential that the plenum is colder than the space due to envelope loads,
the logic leads with the supply air temperature rather than with an increase in fan speed.

manual_entries.title: Parallel Fan Start/Stop for Ventilation (ASHRAE 62.1)
manual_entries.summary: In occupied cooling mode with ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus half Pfan-z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow setpoint equals Voz minus current primary airflow setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Parallel Fan Start/Stop for Ventilation (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload.summary: In occupied cooling mode with ASHRAE 62.1 ventilation, parallel fan starts when primary airflow drops below Voz minus half Pfan-z and shuts off when primary airflow rises above Voz. Fan airflow setpoint equals Voz minus current primary airflow setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Primary airflow < Voz - 0.5*Pfan-z (start); primary airflow > Voz (stop).
structured_payload.required_behavior: Start fan when primary airflow drops below threshold; stop when above; set fan airflow to Voz minus primary airflow setpoint.

manual_entries.title: Heating Mode Sequence for Parallel Fan-Powered Terminal Unit
manual_entries.summary: Active primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint. Parallel fan runs. Heating loop output 0-50% resets discharge temperature from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint. Heating loop output 50-100% resets parallel fan airflow setpoint from deadband value (Pfan-z if less) up to Pfan-htgmax. Heating coil modulates to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.

structured_payload.title: Heating Mode Sequence for Parallel Fan-Powered Terminal Unit
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Parallel fan-powered VAV terminal unit with electric or hot-water reheat
structured_payload.summary: Active primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint. Parallel fan runs. Heating loop output 0-50% resets discharge temperature from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint. Heating loop output 50-100% resets parallel fan airflow setpoint from deadband value (Pfan-z if less) up to Pfan-htgmax. Heating coil modulates to maintain discharge temperature setpoint.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone state = heating.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Set primary airflow to minimum; run fan; reset discharge temp and fan airflow as per heating loop output.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: From 0 % to 50%, the Heating Loop output shall reset the discharge temperature from the current AHU SAT setpoint to a maximum of MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint.

exceeds the minimum required airflow for the electric heater.

current AHU SAT setpoint  to a maximum of MaxT above space temperature setpoint .
ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1 -2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C

manual_entries.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
manual_entries.summary: If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

structured_payload.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload.summary: If zone temp exceeds cooling setpoint by 3°C for 2 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C for 2 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if cooling loop > 95%, send 1 request until < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature or cooling loop output thresholds.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send integer requests (0-3) based on conditions.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes ... send 3 requests.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  85 Per Section 5.1.11 , all hardware points can be overridden  through the BAS. Each of the following points is
interlocked so that they can be overridden together at a zone -group level, per Section 5.4.5 .
For example, the CxA can check for leaking dampers by forcing all VAV boxes in a Zone Group  closed and then
recording airflow at the AHU.
Central plant sequences are not part of the initial scope of Guideline 36, but control logic for plant requests are
being included for future use, when central plant sequences are added.
Typically, the heating hot -water plant will start when there is at least one request for 5 minutes, and stop when there
are no requests for 5 minutes, after a minimum run -time has elapsed.
Hot-water reset requests are used in T&R loops to control supply water temperature and/or pump DP setpoint s
based on zone and AHU demands.

manual_entries.title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit Control Logic
manual_entries.summary: Control logic for cooling, deadband, and heating modes. In cooling, cooling loop output maps to primary airflow setpoint between minimum and cooling maximum. In deadband, setpoint is minimum. In heating, setpoint is minimum and heating loop resets discharge temperature from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space setpoint. VAV damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint. Fan runs when zone is in heating/cooling or zone group is occupied; prior to start, damper closes fully for 15 seconds then releases.

structured_payload.title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit Control Logic
structured_payload.section_title: Series Fan-Powered Terminal Unit − Constant-Volume Fan
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Constant-volume series fan-powered VAV terminal unit
structured_payload.summary: Control logic for cooling, deadband, and heating modes. In cooling, cooling loop output maps to primary airflow setpoint between minimum and cooling maximum. In deadband, setpoint is minimum. In heating, setpoint is minimum and heating loop resets discharge temperature from AHU SAT to MaxΔT above space setpoint. VAV damper modulates to maintain measured airflow at setpoint. Fan runs when zone is in heating/cooling or zone group is occupied; prior to start, damper closes fully for 15 seconds then releases.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone state changes (cooling, deadband, heating) or occupancy mode changes.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Map cooling loop output to active primary airflow setpoint between minimum and cooling maximum endpoints; in deadband set to minimum; in heating set to minimum and reset discharge temperature; modulate damper to maintain airflow setpoint; run fan per mode; close damper before fan start for 15 seconds.

manual_entries.title: Fan Start Sequence
manual_entries.summary: Fan runs whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is driven fully closed to ensure fan is not rotating backward. Once fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.

structured_payload.title: Fan Start Sequence
structured_payload.summary: Fan runs whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone state is heating or cooling, or zone group mode is Occupied.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Close damper fully before fan start; wait 15 seconds after fan proven ON; then release damper override.

manual_entries.title: Testing/Commissioning Overrides
manual_entries.summary: Provide software switches interlocked to system level point to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, or Vmin; force damper full closed/open; force heating ON/closed; turn fan ON/OFF; reset request-hours accumulator. All hardware points can be overridden through BAS; overrides can be applied at zone-group level.

manual_entries.title: Snap-Acting Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit Control Logic
manual_entries.summary: When Zone State is cooling, cooling-loop output resets active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold damper modulates to maintain setpoint; hot damper closed. When deadband, active setpoints hold last values (snap-action). When heating, heating-loop output resets active hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot damper modulates; cold damper closed.

structured_payload.title: Snap-Acting Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit Control Logic
structured_payload.section_title: Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload.equipment_scope: Dual-duct VAV terminal unit with dual inlet airflow sensors
structured_payload.summary: When Zone State is cooling, cooling-loop output resets active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold damper modulates to maintain setpoint; hot damper closed. When deadband, active setpoints hold last values (snap-action). When heating, heating-loop output resets active hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot damper modulates; cold damper closed.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone State changes between cooling, deadband, and heating
structured_payload.required_behavior: Cooling mode: cold duct setpoint = min to Vcool-max, cold damper modulates, hot damper closed. Deadband: maintain last setpoints (snap-action). Heating mode: hot duct setpoint = min to Vheat-max, hot damper modulates, cold damper closed. If cold-deck SAT > room temp, cold setpoint ≤ min. If hot-deck SAT < room temp, hot setpoint ≤ min.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: When the Zone State is cooling, the cooling-loop output shall reset the active cold duct airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint.

reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

alarm.

manual_entries.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
manual_entries.summary: If fan serving zone is off and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of max airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload.summary: If fan serving zone is off and airflow sensor reading > max(10% of max airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Fan off and sensor reading above threshold for 30 minutes
structured_payload.required_behavior: Generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

chunk.cleaned_text:
ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  97 5.11.6.2.  Airflow Sensor Calibration . If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor readin g is above
the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3
alarm.
5.11.6.3.  Leaking Damper.  If the damper position is 0 %, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the
damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure air flow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
5.11.7.  Testing/Commissioning Overrides . Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c.

damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow.

manual_entries.title: Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit System Requests - Heating SAT Reset
manual_entries.summary: Send 3 requests if zone temp below heating setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes after suppression; else send 2 requests if below by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes; else send 1 request if Heating Loop > 95% until < 85%; else send 0 requests.

structured_payload.title: Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit System Requests - Heating SAT Reset
structured_payload.summary: Send 3 requests if zone temp below heating setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes after suppression; else send 2 requests if below by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes; else send 1 request if Heating Loop > 95% until < 85%; else send 0 requests.
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Zone temperature deviation or Heating Loop output
structured_payload.required_behavior: Send integer request count (0-3) to heating SAT reset.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: If the zone temperature is below the zone’s heating setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

chunk.cleaned_text:
98 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.11.8.3.  Heating SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature is below the zone’s heating setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature is below the zone’ s heating setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Heating Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Heating Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Heating Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests

manual_entries.title: Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit Temperature Control
manual_entries.summary: In cooling mode, cooling loop output resets cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold damper modulates to maintain setpoint. If cold deck SAT > room temp, setpoint capped at minimum. In deadband, cold duct setpoint = minimum, cold damper maintains airflow, hot damper closed. In heating mode, heating loop output resets hot duct airflow setpoint from zero to heating maximum; hot damper modulates to maintain setpoint; cold damper maintains sum of inlet airflows at minimum. If hot deck SAT < room temp, hot duct setpoint capped at minimum.

In heating mode, heating loop output resets hot duct airflow setpoint from zero to heating maximum; hot damper modulates to maintain setpoint; cold damper maintains sum of inlet airflows at minimum. If hot deck SAT < room temp, hot duct setpoint capped at minimum.

manual_entries.summary: Cooling damper uses pressure-independent control; heating damper uses pressure-dependent control. In cooling mode, cooling loop resets cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold duct damper modulates to maintain setpoint. In deadband, cold duct setpoint is minimum, hot duct closed. In heating, heating loop maps to hot duct damper position; cold duct damper maintains discharge at minimum setpoint.

ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  103 5.13.5.  Control logic is depicted schematically in Figure 5.13.5  and described in the following subsections.
Figure 5.13.5 Control logic for mixing dual -duct VAV zone with discharge sensor.
5.13.5.1.  Temperature Control
Because there is only a single airflow sensor on the combined discharge, typical pressure -independent control will
not work for both dampers. Instead, the cooling damper is controlled using pressure -independent control, while the
heating damper position equals the Heating Loop  signal (i.e., pressure -dependent control).
a. When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active cold duct  airflow

shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cold duct airflow at the active

structured_payload.title: Hot Duct Damper Position Limiting
structured_payload.summary: Maximum hot duct airflow is limited by a reverse-acting P-only loop with setpoint equal to heating maximum endpoint; output is maximum hot duct damper position (0-100%).
structured_payload.trigger_condition: Heating mode active.
structured_payload.required_behavior: Limit hot duct damper position to prevent airflow exceeding Vheat-max using reverse-acting P-only loop.
structured_payload.evidence_quote: Maximum hot duct airflow shall be limited by a reverse-acting P-only loop whose setpoint is the heating maximum endpoint and whose output is the maximum hot duct damper position ranging from 0% to 100%.

position ranging from 0% to 100%.
Because the heating damper is operating in a pressure -dependent manner, a loop must be added to limit heating
damper position to Vheat -max. When this comes into pla y, the only air passing through the discharge airflow sensor
is heating air.
